units.title: Health, Safety and Environment in Automotive industry

learning_outcomes.description: Carryout Personal health and hygiene

performance_criteria.description: Wear clean, smart and appropriate personal protective equipment (wears).
Work safely at all times, complying with health, safety and environmental regulations and guidelines.
Get cuts, grazes and wounds treated by the appropriate personnel.
Report any form of illness promptly to the appropriate personnel.

learning_outcomes.description: Maintain personal health and hygiene

performance_criteria.description: Identify own responsibility in the health and safety Act as it relates to own occupation.
Identify general rules on hygiene that must be followed.
Identify correct personal protection equipment (such as Head Protection, Foot Protection, Hand and body protection) and regulatory protection.
Identify the importance of maintaining good personal hygiene.
Demonstrate how to deal with cuts, grazes and wounds and why it is important to do so.
Identify the importance of working in a healthy, safe and hygienic workplace.
Report any accidents or near misses quickly and accurately to the proper personnel.
Identify health, hygiene and safety procedure at work.
Carryout emergency procedures during work.
Identify organizational security procedures and measures.
Carryout disposal of waste and pollution control with organic and inorganic waste disposal methods.
Follow noise control and protection methods.
Identify any potential hazards and deal with these correctly.
Identify where information about health, safety and environment in the workplace can be obtained.
Identify the types of hazards in the workplace that may occur and how to deal with them.
Identify hazards that can be dealt with personally and those that should be reported to the appropriate personnel.
Identify how to warn other people about potential hazards/hazards and why this is important.
Identify why accidents and near- accidents should be reported and to whom.
Identify the types of emergencies that may happen in the workplace and how to deal with it.
Identify where to find the first- aid equipment and who the registered first responder is in the work place
Carryout safe lifting and handling techniques that should be followed.
Identify other ways of working safely that are relevant to own position and why they are important.
Carryout organizational emergency procedures, in particular fire, and how these should be followed.
Identify the possible causes of fire and how to minimize the possibility of fire in the workplace.
Identify where to find the alarms and how to set them off.
Identify the importance of following the fire safety laws and why it should never be approached unless it is safe to do so.
Describe the organizational security procedures and why these are important.
Identify the importance of reporting all incidents to the appropriate personnel.
